office_does: The auditor administers elections, records, licensing, county finances, and public-facing statutory services.
race_blurb: Incumbent Lori Larsen offers direct auditor administration, while Eric Simpson presents an explicitly outsider case rooted in industry and household experience.

candidates.summary: Simpson has more than 20 years in welding and logging and emphasizes budgeting, spending priorities, and election-process change.
highlights: Has long industry experience but acknowledges no auditor-duty background [S2]
Opposes Washington vote by mail and proposes election-process changes [S2]

spending.basis: Budgeting and spending priorities suggest oversight, but the dossier gives no concrete restraint mechanism.

experience.basis: He explicitly presents an outsider case without professional experience in the office’s statutory duties.

candidates.summary: Larsen is the incumbent auditor with eight years of administration emphasizing elections, reporting, compliance, records, and responsible public-dollar management.
highlights: Serves as incumbent auditor with eight years of administration [S1][S2]
Emphasizes reporting, compliance, and responsible management [S2]

spending.basis: Financial reporting and responsible management support an accountability orientation.

experience.basis: Incumbency and direct elections, records, and financial-administration experience establish a proven record.
